actionable_feedback: Regenerate the workbook in a parseable format (ensure openpyxl-compatible .xlsx or include an exported CSV/OOXML snippet) so verifiers can inspect formulas and cell provenance.

Embed explicit row-level citations in the Calc/Decision sheets and mirror them in acceptance_letter.md. Use a consistent notation (e.g., 'Calc!B12 = Packet!A3 (packet:P2.pressure_psi)') adjacent to each computed margin and key intermediate value.

Add numeric sensitivity-case output tables immediately next to the controlling calculations (show the margin/result for each perturbation such as P2 +/-1 psi and Tank +/-0.5 h) so reviewers can see results without opening formulas.

Define explicit, actionable retest criteria in the acceptance letter: list which test points to measure in the field, required instrumentation/tolerances, target pass thresholds, and pass/fail decision rules for marginal cases.
Provide at least two alternative action-package comparisons (e.g., minimum-cost package vs full-compliance package) with combined pressure gains, combined costs, and cost-per-psi for each package.

Re-run the deterministic precheck and verifier after the changes and attach a parseable workbook preview or an exported cell-level mapping (sheet+cell+value+formula) to the submission to close the verification loop.

feedback_summary: Deliverables produced and arithmetic corrected; recommendation logical and conservative (conditional acceptance with retest). Primary issues: the .xlsx preview was not parseable in the verifier (openpyxl unavailable), preventing direct cell-level verification of formulas and explicit sheet+cell citations; the acceptance_letter.md does not show explicit row-level citations mapping packet inputs to workbook cells; explicit retest criteria and alternative action-package comparisons are not provided. Sensitivity analysis is documented narratively, but numeric sensitivity outputs are not shown adjacent to controlling calculations in the acceptance letter preview.

raw_llm_output.suggested_revision: Produce 644-revised that (1) outputs an openpyxl-parseable .xlsx, (2) adds explicit sheet+cell citations mapped to packet inputs, (3) places numeric sensitivity tables adjacent to controlling calculations, (4) specifies clear field retest procedures and thresholds, and (5) includes action-package comparisons; then rerun verification and update acceptance_letter.md to reference the workbook cell mapping.
